step1.surface_finish_requirements: l O 0.010 A B C; l O 0.002 A B C; l O .020 A B C

drivers: High feature count (82 holes, 62 bores, 23 threaded holes)
risk_flags: High feature density risking positioning errors

part_summary.notes: Large heavy part ~815 kg (1796.88 lbm). Engraving '1SI0574AM122'. Break sharp edges. Multi-sheet drawing (3/3). Likely cylindrical/turned with milled hole patterns. Zones A,B,C,D.

machining_features.description: CSK at B locations Ø0.563 x 82 deg
Pilot holes at B Ø0.505 x 0.5 in
Ø1.000 min depth for complete thread
Break sharp edges (ENLEVER ARETES COUPANTES); also 44°/52° in section A-A

general_notes: General tolerances (in): Linear X./FRACT. ±1/16, .X ±.040, .XX ±.020, .XXX ±.010; Angular X./FRACT. ±1°, .X ±.5°, .XX ±.25°
Surface finish: Ra 0.010, 0.002, 0.020 (microinches → ~0.25μm, 0.05μm, 0.5μm) in zones A B C
Revision: I.S / VER. a, Released for production 2025-11-19

scenarios.rationale: Large heavy part with high feature density (holes, bores, threads) machined from billet stock on 4-axis CNC boring mill matching size/weight capabilities and part summary (5 setups, 4th axis required). All features handled in sequenced setups: roughing, boring, drilling, tapping, finishing.
assumptions: 1045 steel billet provided near net shape
Part bounding box fits machine envelope (2500x1500x1200 mm) via optimal orientation/fixturing
No pre-machined stock or casting
Cycle times estimated from feature counts and machine defaults (setup 120 min, runtime scaled by complexity)
unknowns: Exact fixturing design for 815 kg part
Location of zones A/B/C/D relative to setups

steps.description: Rough facing, contouring, and initial pockets on primary face
Precision boring of 62 bores with Ra 0.05 finish in zones A/B/C
Drill 59 thru/pilot holes (Type A/C, Ø17/12.8/25.4 mm) with tight tolerances
Tap 23 threaded holes (4x 1/2-13 UNC, 19x 3/8-18 NPT) to depth
CSK 37 countersinks, chamfer all edges, engraving '1SI0574AM122'

scenarios.rationale: Rough plasma cut from 1045 plate stock (avg ~25 mm thick inferred from weight/vol) for profile/hole blanks, followed by CNC for precision bores/threads/finishes. Suits large envelope, reduces CNC roughing time/load; welding skipped as none specified.
assumptions: Part is prismatic/plate-like with thin walls allowing plasma (max 50 mm)
Plasma roughs large contours/holes >8 mm dia
Post-plasma distortion corrected in CNC
Multiple plasma sheets if needed for size

steps.description: Rough cut profile, large holes (Ø17+, pilots, type C), breakouts for bores
Face plasma blank, stress relieve, rough pockets for bores access
Precision bores (62), drill remaining holes, CSK
Tap 23 threads, chamfer edges, engraving

part_summary.notes: CHROMER 0.005" to 0.010". Bottom anvil smooth (USINAGE LISSE D'ENCLUME DU BAS). Weight 35.341 lbm. T = thinnest thickness.

machining_features.description: Machined flat faces including bottom anvil smooth surface
Perimeter profile with steps and large arc Ø26 5/16"
Stepped pockets/slots per drawing dimensions (36.250", 31.100", etc.)
1/4" x 50° chamfers and 110° angles per details

scenarios.rationale: Plasma rough cut for efficient material removal on thick steel plate, followed by multi-setup CNC boring mill for precision features (faces, contours, pockets, holes, threads, chamfers) matching machine envelope and capabilities, then chrome plating. Matches part size (1010x240x64mm, 16kg), 6 machined faces, tight tolerances.
assumptions: Shop provides CHT-100 steel plate stock
Plasma table can handle 63.5mm thickness (near limit, slow speeds assumed)
Standard fixturing for large part with 4th axis indexing if needed
Chrome plating outsourced or in-house with standard times
unknowns: Exact plasma cut speed for 63.5mm steel
Precise material removal volume for cycle times
Plating thickness uniformity on complex geometry

steps.description: Rough plasma cut perimeter contour, steps, and rough pockets/slots from stock plate (perimeter ~2.5m, thick material)
Setup 1: Fixture rough blank, face mill bottom anvil smooth and primary faces (1000x240mm area)
Setup 2: Re-fixture/flip, machine 5 stepped pockets/slots (high volume removal, depths per drawing)
Setup 3: External contour profiling, steps/shoulders, large arc, chamfers/bevels (Ø668mm arc, ±0.002", ±0.5° angles)
Setup 4: Drill 6x Ø13/32" holes + counterbores, 2x pilot + 3/8-16 UNC threads (pattern alignment critical)
Chrome plating 0.005"-0.010" thick (clean, plate, rinse, bake; full envelope immersion)

scenarios.rationale: Single machine group for all operations starting from stock plate, avoiding plasma thickness limit and cut distortions; extended roughing cycle due to ~100kg removal but higher precision control.
assumptions: CNC boring mill equipped for heavy roughing (high torque, large tools)
Stock plate fixtured securely for initial roughing
No need for plasma precision issues
unknowns: Roughing feed rates and tool life for massive removal

steps.description: Rough mill contour, rough pockets/slots, step shoulders from stock plate (major material removal ~118kg)
Finish face mill all 6 faces including bottom anvil smooth (±0.002")
Finish pockets/slots, external contour, arc, steps/chamfers (tight tolerances)
Drill/counterbore 6 holes, thread 2 holes (high precision)
Chrome plating 0.005"-0.010" thick

risk_flags: Distortion risk on large thin plate

part_summary.notes: Irregular plate shape with radiused corners; 7 surfaces chromed 0.005"-0.010"; Oxy-cut base with CNC finishing; Mirror variant required (qty 2 total)

machining_features.description: Oxy-cut and machined perimeter with radii R7.938" (201.7mm), R8.562" (217.4mm), angles 61°,65°
Drill Ø11/16" (17.5mm) with countersink Ø1-1/4" (31.75mm) x 82°
Drill Ø13/32" (10.3mm) with chamfer Ø21/32" (16.7mm) x 25/64" (9.9mm) deep

machining_features.location: Multiple edges, sections B-B, C-C, D, E

additional_operations: Chrome plating on 7 surfaces: 0.005" to 0.010" thick
general_notes: General tolerances: Dimensions xx ±0.005", xxx ±0.002", angles ±0.5°

scenarios.rationale: Efficient routing starting with plasma cutting for rough irregular perimeter on large plate stock, followed by comprehensive CNC finishing on 4-axis boring mill (suitable for part envelope 366x323x25mm, weight 6kg, 4 setups for 7 faces/holes/chamfers), then chrome plating on 7 surfaces. Times aggregated from feature estimates (total CNC ~49min cycle) and machine defaults, shared setup for qty=2 (1 normal +1 mirror).
assumptions: Setups shared across qty=2 parts
CNC_BORING_LARGE_4AX can handle CHT-100 steel plate with tight tolerances (±0.002")
Chrome plating is post-machining external process with standard lead time
4 CNC setups included in single machining step time totals
Oxy-cut approximated by plasma capabilities (25.4mm thickness within max 50mm)
unknowns: Exact fixturing design for 4 setups and thin plate distortion prevention
Precise chrome plating cycle time and thickness uniformity on chromed faces

steps.description: Rough cut irregular perimeter (radii R201.7mm, R217.4mm, angles 61°/65°) from stock plate, including lead-in/out
4 setups: Face mill 7 surfaces, finish perimeter contour, drill 4x Ø17.5mm CSK holes + 4x Ø10.3mm chamfer holes, edge chamfers (e.g. 5/8x45°, 3/16x65°), tight tolerances ±0.002"/±0.005" positional
Chrome plate 7 machined surfaces (top/bottom/5 sides) to 0.005"-0.010" thick

scenarios.rationale: No plasma/oxy-cut; full roughing and finishing of contour, faces, holes, chamfers in CNC to avoid cut-edge quality issues, using large 4-axis boring mill. Longer cycle due to roughing but potentially better precision. Suitable if plasma availability limited.
assumptions: CNC can efficiently rough large perimeter without burning/distortion on 25mm steel
Increased cycle accounts for roughing passes (~+30min)
Same chrome post-process
Qty=2 sharing setups
Material removal rates per machine capabilities (steel, tight tol multiplier 1.25)
unknowns: Roughing tool life and cycle extension for full contour rough/finish
Distortion risk without pre-cut blank (higher thin plate flex)

steps.description: Setup 1-2: Rough and finish perimeter contour, face mill primary faces, initial chamfers (4 setups total)
Setup 3-4: Drill/counterbore/chamfer all 8 holes, finish remaining 7 chromable faces to ±0.002"
Chrome plate 7 machined surfaces (top/bottom/5 sides) to 0.005"-0.010" thick

drivers: high hole count (12 total incl. threaded/ bores)

part_summary.notes: Elongated thin prismatic key steel part. Multiple holes along length. Tight general tolerances (±0.002" on 3-place dims, geometric ±0.002"). Fixturing risk for long part (L/H >15).

machining_features.description: Overall rectangular prismatic contour 1-1/4 x 11-1/4 x 20-1/2 inch key bar

machining_features.location: along centerline spaced per dims (0.5" to 18.625")

scenarios.scenario_name: Baseline Plasma Rough + Multi-Setup CNC Milling
scenarios.rationale: Elongated prismatic steel part with tight tolerances, multiple holes, bores, threads, and slot. Plasma for efficient rough blanking from plate stock, followed by 4 setups on large 4-axis CNC boring mill to handle fixturing challenges, machined faces, hole patterns, and precision features. Matches 6 machined faces with grouped operations.
assumptions: Customer provides plate stock thicker than 40mm
5mm machining allowance on plasma cut edges
Holes drilled from primary faces into thickness
Standard HSS or carbide tools for steel
No special fixturing beyond soft jaws/clamps
Quantity=1, no batch efficiencies
unknowns: Exact hole depths and positions requiring probing
Optimal fixturing to avoid vibration on thin long part

steps.description: Plasma cut rough rectangular blank (530x295x40mm + allowance) from steel plate
Setup 1: Fixture on large face (520x286mm), face mill top, mill slot/groove (12.7mm wide), external contour roughing, chamfers
Flip/reclamp: Face mill bottom face, finish contour
Setup 2: Re-fixture for hole access, drill 10x Ø7.14mm holes + 8x countersinks, position per pattern
Setup 3: Bore/ream 2x Ø15.48mm x50.8mm deep holes, tap 2x 3/8-18 NPT, final inspection/probing for tolerances

scenarios.scenario_name: Alternative Full CNC Milling from Oversized Stock
scenarios.rationale: No plasma; machine directly from larger customer-supplied stock (e.g., 600x300x50mm). Increased cycle times for roughing but avoids plasma heat affect zone. Still uses 4-axis CNC for efficiency on prismatic features.
assumptions: Larger raw stock provided (extra 50mm all sides)
Extended roughing cycles acceptable
Same fixturing as baseline
No distortion from plasma HAZ
unknowns: Additional roughing time impact on total lead time

steps.description: Setup 1: Fixture on large face, rough/face mill top, rough contour/sides, mill slot/chamfers
Flip/reclamp: Rough/face bottom, finish contour
Setup 2: Drill 10x holes + countersinks in pattern
Setup 3: Bore 2x deep holes, NPT tapping, final GD&T check

scenarios.rationale: Large simple part with no detected features and significant weight (17kg) suggests a flat plate profile cut from stock material using plasma table, suitable for undefined envelope within 3000x1500mm.
assumptions: Part is flat steel plate under 50mm thick
Single operation for rough profile
Density ~7.8g/cm³ steel implied by weight

steps.description: Cut part profile from steel plate stock

scenarios.rationale: Heavy part (17kg) with unknown shape and low complexity indicates possible welded assembly from cut plates or bars, using standard fab bay for large envelope up to 8000kg.
assumptions: Part is welded from multiple simple pieces
Includes fitting, welding, grinding
Steel material

steps.description: Plasma or saw cut raw plates/bars
Fit, weld, and grind assembly

scenarios.rationale: Fallback for prismatic block with minimal features; large 4-axis CNC can handle 17kg part within 2500x1500x1200mm envelope, assuming rough facing or simple boring.
assumptions: Rectangular stock block
Minimal machining: facing and basic features
Steel or compatible material

steps.description: Rough mill faces and basic profile on large 4-axis

Title: RENFORT RAIL LINEAIRE PRESSE A PLAT HWAYI 900T

scenarios.rationale: Plasma rough cut for efficient stepped profile from plate stock, followed by 4 CNC setups on large 4-axis boring mill for precision faces, hole drilling, counterbores, and threading. Matches part envelope (1425x76x45mm, 126kg), high hole count (35+10 threaded), tight tolerances, and imperial units. Post-ops for deburr, paint, and anti-rust per notes.
assumptions: Plate stock 1450x80x50mm steel 44W available
4 setups sufficient for all features with standard fixturing (e.g., modular clamps along length)
Holes primarily accessible from top/sides post-face milling
Per-part times; no qty specified
Painting excludes cure/bake time from cycle
Machines capable of imperial programming/tolerances
unknowns: Exact fixturing design and hole orientations/depths
Potential distortion from plasma requiring extra stock

steps.description: Rough cut stepped linear rail profile from 50mm steel plate stock including rough outline for large clearance hole positions
Setup 1: Fixture on rough face, mill precision top face to 1.778in (+0.002/-0.000) stepped thickness, partial contour cleanup
Setup 2: Flip/reclamp, mill precision bottom face to 1.772in (+0.000/-0.002) stepped thickness
Setup 3: Refixture for sides, mill side/end faces, drill 25 large 14mm clearance holes (pos tol 0.01in ABC)
Setup 4: Same/adjust fixture, drill 10 small 6.07mm holes w/ counterbores (14.5x20mm), tap 10x 1/2-13 UNC threads (pos tol 0.002in ABC)
Manual deburr/chamfer all edges, holes, threads per general note 'remove sharp edges'
Mask threads/machined surfaces, apply JC-JAUNE Caterpillar paint (5gal #4C6122 /1gal #4C4200), anti-rust on unpainted areas

scenarios.rationale: Rough and finish entirely on large 4-axis CNC from oversized plate stock (e.g., 1500x100x60mm). Avoids potential plasma HAZ/distortion but increases roughing time and tool wear for heavy steel removal. Suitable if plasma unavailable or precision roughing needed.
assumptions: Oversized plate stock available
Additional roughing cycle feasible within machine envelope/power
Same 4 finish setups post-roughing
No plasma-related cleanup needed
Per-part times
unknowns: Exact roughing toolpath length and material removal volume (~20-30kg)
Fixturing stability for roughing heavy cuts
Cycle time impact of conservative feeds on long part

steps.description: Rough mill exterior stepped profile, faces, and pockets from oversized plate to near-net leaving 2-3mm stock
Setup 1: Mill precision top face to 1.778in stepped
Setup 2: Flip, mill bottom face to 1.772in stepped
Setup 3: Sides, mill faces, drill 25x14mm clearance holes
Setup 4: Drill small holes, counterbores, tap threads
Manual deburr/chamfer all features
Mask, paint JC-JAUNE, apply anti-rust

part_summary.notes: Weld Tool Bottom Plate; large thin plate with high hole density; thru holes; reamed bores

scenarios.rationale: Plasma rough cut for efficient blanking of large thin aluminum plate, followed by 2-setup CNC machining for high hole density and precision bores on suitable large boring machine, ending with anodize. Matches part size, features, and 2-setup estimate.
assumptions: Single prototype quantity
Material supplied as stock plate
Thru holes accessible from one side primarily
Anodize is clear type I/II rack process
No special fixturing needed beyond vacuum/clamps
unknowns: Raw stock availability and sawing if no plasma

steps.description: Rough plasma cut perimeter with R12 allowance from 1410x960x10 stock plate
Setup 1: Face top surface, finish contour perimeter, drill 54 holes (7mm,9mm,12mm), countersink
Setup 2: Flip part, face bottom surface, ream 12 high-precision bores, break all edges
Clear anodize full part to 3.2um equivalent finish

scenarios.rationale: Fully machined on single large CNC boring machine without plasma; assumes pre-sawn stock blank. Higher cost but better edge quality and no heat distortion risk from plasma on thin Al plate.
assumptions: Stock plate pre-cut to ~1450x1000x10 via saw/shear
All features including rough contour done in CNC setups
Adequate fixturing for thin plate to prevent warp
unknowns: Potential warping from larger stock milling

steps.description: Setup 1: Rough face top, rough contour to near net with material allowance
Setup 2: Finish face top, drill all holes, countersink
Setup 3: Flip, face bottom, ream bores, finish contour and break edges
Clear anodize full part
